401.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'O Messenger of Allah, we were in evil, then Allah removed that evil and brought good through you. Is there evil after this good?' He said: 'Yes.' He said: 'What is it?' He said: 'Trials like the darkness of night, one following another, coming upon you as alike as the faces of cows — you will not know one from another.'
401.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, kami berada dalam keburukan, kemudian Allah menghilangkan keburukan itu dan mendatangkan kebaikan melaluimu. Apakah ada keburukan setelah kebaikan ini?' Beliau bersabda: 'Ya.' Dia berkata: 'Apa itu?' Beliau bersabda: 'Fitnah-fitnah seperti kegelapan malam, satu mengikuti yang lain, datang kepada kalian menyerupai wajah-wajah sapi — kalian tidak akan bisa membedakan satu dari yang lain.'

402.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'My mother asked me: "How long has it been since you last visited the Prophet ﷺ?" I told her such and such. She rebuked and berated me. I said: "Leave me, for I will go to the Prophet ﷺ and pray Maghrib with him, then I will not leave him until he asks forgiveness for me and for you." So I came to the Prophet ﷺ, prayed Maghrib with him...' and he described how the Prophet ﷺ prayed for him and his mother.
402.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Ibuku bertanya kepadaku: "Sudah berapa lama kamu tidak mengunjungi Nabi ﷺ?" Aku memberitahunya demikian dan demikian. Dia memarahiku dan mencercaku. Aku berkata: "Biarkan aku, karena aku akan pergi kepada Nabi ﷺ dan shalat Maghrib bersamanya, lalu aku tidak akan meninggalkannya hingga beliau memohonkan ampunan untuk aku dan untukmu." Maka aku mendatangi Nabi ﷺ, shalat Maghrib bersamanya...' dan dia menceritakan bagaimana Nabi ﷺ mendoakan dia dan ibunya.

403.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I came to the Prophet ﷺ and prayed Zuhr, Asr, Maghrib, and Isha with him. Then I followed him when he was about to enter one of his rooms, and he stood while I was behind him as if speaking to someone. Then he said: "Who is this?" I said: "Hudhayfa." He said: "Do you know who was with me?" I said: "No." He said: "That was Jibril who came to give me the good news that al-Hasan and al-Husayn are the masters of the youth of Paradise." Hudhayfa said: "Then ask forgiveness for me."'
403.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Aku mendatangi Nabi ﷺ dan shalat Dzuhur, Ashar, Maghrib, dan Isya bersamanya. Kemudian aku mengikutinya ketika beliau hendak masuk ke salah satu kamarnya, dan beliau berdiri sementara aku ada di belakangnya seolah berbicara dengan seseorang. Kemudian beliau berkata: "Siapa ini?" Aku berkata: "Hudzaifah." Beliau berkata: "Apakah kamu tahu siapa yang bersamaku?" Aku berkata: "Tidak." Beliau berkata: "Itu adalah Jibril yang datang membawa kabar gembira bahwa al-Hasan dan al-Husain adalah pemimpin para pemuda surga." Hudzaifah berkata: "Mohonkan ampunan untuk aku."'

404.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man passed by and it was said: 'This man reports news to the rulers.' Hudhayfa said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "A tale-bearer will not enter Paradise."'
404.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki lewat dan dikatakan: 'Laki-laki ini melaporkan berita kepada para penguasa.' Hudzaifah berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Penyebar fitnah tidak akan masuk surga."'

405.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'I was brought al-Buraq, a long white animal that places its hoof at the farthest point of its gaze. Jibril (peace be upon him) and I did not dismount from its back until we came to Jerusalem. Then the gates of heaven were opened for us and we saw Paradise and the Fire.' Hudhayfa added: 'And he did not pray in Jerusalem.' Zirr said: 'I said to him: "Indeed he did pray."'
405.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Aku dibawa al-Buraq, seekor hewan putih yang panjang yang meletakkan tapaknya di ujung pandangannya. Jibril (alaihissalam) dan aku tidak turun dari punggungnya hingga kami tiba di Baitul Maqdis. Kemudian pintu-pintu langit dibukakan untuk kami dan kami melihat surga dan neraka.' Hudzaifah menambahkan: 'Dan beliau tidak shalat di Baitul Maqdis.' Zirr berkata: 'Aku berkata kepadanya: "Sesungguhnya beliau memang shalat."'

406.iwh_en: It was narrated from Muhammad ibn Ka'b al-Qurazi that a young man from Kufa said to Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman: 'O Abu Abdillah, you saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and accompanied him?' He said: 'Yes, O my nephew.' He asked: 'How did you act with him?' Hudhayfa said: 'By Allah, we made great effort.' The young man said: 'By Allah, if we had been there we would not have let him walk on the ground — we would have carried him on our necks.' Hudhayfa replied: 'O my nephew, by Allah, I saw us at the Battle of the Trench...' and he described the hardships they faced.
406.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Muhammad ibn Ka'b al-Quradzi bahwa seorang pemuda dari Kufah berkata kepada Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman: 'Wahai Abu Abdillah, Anda telah melihat Rasulullah ﷺ dan menemaninya?' Dia berkata: 'Ya, wahai keponakanku.' Dia bertanya: 'Bagaimana kalian bersikap kepadanya?' Hudzaifah berkata: 'Demi Allah, kami benar-benar bersungguh-sungguh.' Pemuda itu berkata: 'Demi Allah, seandainya kami ada di sana, kami tidak akan membiarkannya berjalan di atas tanah — kami akan membawanya di atas pundak kami.' Hudzaifah menjawab: 'Wahai keponakanku, demi Allah, aku melihat kami pada Perang Khandaq...' dan dia menggambarkan kesulitan yang mereka hadapi.

407.iwh_en: It was narrated from Rib'i ibn Hirash that he was at the funeral of Hudhayfa when a man from the people said: 'I heard this man — meaning Hudhayfa — say: "I have no fear regarding what I heard from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. And if you fight one another, I will go to the furthest room in my house and enter it. If someone enters upon me I will say: Take upon yourself my sin and your sin."'
407.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Rib'i ibn Hirasy bahwa dia berada di pemakaman Hudzaifah ketika seorang dari kaum berkata: 'Aku mendengar orang ini — yaitu Hudzaifah — berkata: "Aku tidak khawatir tentang apa yang aku dengar dari Rasulullah ﷺ. Dan jika kalian saling berperang, aku akan pergi ke kamar paling jauh di rumahku dan memasukinya. Jika seseorang memasukinya aku akan berkata: Tanggungkan atas dirimu dosaku dan dosamu."'

408.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ was absent from us one day and did not come out until we thought he would not come out. When he came out he made one prostration, and we thought his soul had been taken during it. When he raised his head he said: "My Lord — Blessed and Most High — consulted me about my nation, asking what I would do with them. I said: "Whatever You will, O my Lord — they are Your creation and Your servants." He consulted me a second time...' and he mentioned the mercy Allah showed for his nation.
408.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ tidak terlihat oleh kami selama sehari dan tidak keluar hingga kami mengira beliau tidak akan keluar. Ketika beliau keluar, beliau melakukan satu sujud, dan kami mengira jiwanya telah diambil di dalamnya. Ketika beliau mengangkat kepalanya, beliau bersabda: "Tuhanku — Tabaraka wa Ta'ala — bermusyawarah denganku tentang umatku, menanyakan apa yang akan aku lakukan dengan mereka. Aku berkata: "Apa pun yang Engkau kehendaki, ya Tuhanku — mereka adalah ciptaan-Mu dan hamba-hamba-Mu." Beliau bermusyawarah denganku untuk kedua kalinya..." dan beliau menyebutkan rahmat yang Allah tunjukkan untuk umatnya.

409.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Masud and Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with them) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'I will precede you at the Fountain, waiting for you. Men from among you will be raised up to me, and when I recognize them they will be seized away from me. I will say: "O Lord, my Companions! My Companions!" And it will be said: "You do not know what they introduced after you."'
409.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Masud dan Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Aku akan mendahului kalian di telaga, menunggu kalian. Beberapa laki-laki dari antara kalian akan diangkat kepadaku, dan ketika aku mengenali mereka, mereka akan direnggut dariku. Aku akan berkata: "Ya Rabb, para Sahabatku! Para Sahabatku!" Dan akan dikatakan: "Engkau tidak tahu apa yang mereka ada-adakan setelahmu."'

410.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'I know what is with the Dajjal better than he does. With him is a fire that burns, and a river of cold water. Whoever among you encounters him should not be destroyed by him — let him close his eyes and fall into the one that appears to be fire, for it is a river of cold water.'
410.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Aku lebih mengetahui apa yang ada pada Dajjal daripada dia sendiri. Bersamanya ada api yang membakar, dan sungai air yang dingin. Siapa pun di antara kalian yang menemuinya janganlah binasa karenanya — hendaklah dia memejamkan matanya dan jatuh ke dalam yang tampak seperti api, karena sesungguhnya itu adalah sungai air yang dingin.'

411.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: 'I saw in a dream that I met some People of the Book who said: "You are a fine people, if only you did not say: What Allah wills and Muhammad wills."' The Prophet ﷺ said: 'I had already disliked this among you. Say: What Allah wills, then what Muhammad wills.'
411.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki datang kepada Nabi ﷺ dan berkata: 'Aku bermimpi bertemu sebagian Ahli Kitab yang berkata: "Kalian adalah kaum yang baik, seandainya kalian tidak mengucapkan: Apa yang dikehendaki Allah dan Muhammad."' Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Aku sudah tidak menyukai ini dari kalian. Katakanlah: Apa yang dikehendaki Allah, kemudian apa yang dikehendaki Muhammad.'

412.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'My tongue was sharp toward my household, and it did not extend beyond them. I mentioned that to the Prophet ﷺ and he said: "Where are you regarding seeking forgiveness, O Hudhayfa? Indeed I seek forgiveness from Allah one hundred times every day and repent to Him."' He mentioned this to Abu Burda ibn Abi Musa, who said that Abu Musa narrated that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'I seek forgiveness from Allah one hundred times every day and night.'
412.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Lidahku tajam terhadap keluargaku, dan itu tidak melampaui mereka. Aku menyebutkan itu kepada Nabi ﷺ dan beliau bersabda: "Di mana posisimu dalam hal memohon ampun, wahai Hudzaifah? Sesungguhnya aku memohon ampun kepada Allah seratus kali setiap hari dan bertaubat kepada-Nya."' Dia menyebutkan ini kepada Abu Burda ibn Abi Musa, yang berkata bahwa Abu Musa meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Aku memohon ampun kepada Allah seratus kali setiap hari dan malam.'

413.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The person most similar to Muhammad ﷺ in conduct, bearing, and demeanor is Abdullah ibn Masud, from when he goes out until he returns. As for what he does in his house, I do not know.'
413.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Orang yang paling mirip dengan Muhammad ﷺ dalam perilaku, penampilan, dan sikap adalah Abdullah ibn Masud, dari sejak dia keluar hingga dia kembali. Adapun apa yang dia lakukan di rumahnya, aku tidak tahu.'

414.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) through Shaqiq that he said: 'I was sitting with Hudhayfa when Abdullah ibn Masud came. Hudhayfa said: "The person most similar in conduct and bearing to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, from when he leaves his house until he returns — and I do not know what he does among his family — is Abdullah ibn Masud. By Allah, the noble Companions of Muhammad ﷺ all know that Abdullah is among the closest of them to Allah in means of approach on the Day of Resurrection."'
414.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melalui Syaqiq bahwa dia berkata: 'Aku sedang duduk bersama Hudzaifah ketika Abdullah ibn Masud datang. Hudzaifah berkata: "Orang yang paling mirip dalam perilaku dan penampilan dengan Rasulullah ﷺ, dari sejak dia keluar dari rumahnya hingga dia kembali — dan aku tidak tahu apa yang dia lakukan di antara keluarganya — adalah Abdullah ibn Masud. Demi Allah, para Sahabat Muhammad ﷺ yang terpilih semuanya tahu bahwa Abdullah adalah yang paling dekat di antara mereka kepada Allah dalam hal sarana mendekatkan diri pada hari Kiamat."'

415.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was brought al-Buraq, a long white animal that places its hoof at the farthest point of its gaze. He and Jibril did not dismount from its back until they came to Jerusalem. The gates of heaven were opened for them and they saw Paradise and the Fire. Hudhayfa said: 'And he did not pray in Jerusalem.' Zirr said: 'I said: "Indeed he did pray." Hudhayfa said: "What is your name, O bald one? I recognize your face but do not know your name."'
415.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ dibawa al-Buraq, seekor hewan putih yang panjang yang meletakkan tapaknya di ujung pandangannya. Dia dan Jibril tidak turun dari punggungnya hingga mereka tiba di Baitul Maqdis. Pintu-pintu langit dibukakan untuk mereka dan mereka melihat surga dan neraka. Hudzaifah berkata: 'Dan beliau tidak shalat di Baitul Maqdis.' Zirr berkata: 'Aku berkata: "Sesungguhnya beliau shalat." Hudzaifah berkata: "Siapa namamu, wahai orang yang botak? Aku mengenali wajahmu namun tidak tahu namamu."'

416.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he prayed with the Prophet ﷺ and the Prophet ﷺ used to say in his bowing: 'Glory be to my Lord, the Mighty,' and in his prostration: 'Glory be to my Lord, the Most High.' And whenever he came to a verse of mercy he would pause and ask, and whenever he came to a verse of punishment he would seek refuge in Allah.
416.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia shalat bersama Nabi ﷺ dan Nabi ﷺ biasa mengucapkan dalam rukuknya: 'Subhana Rabbiya al-Adzim (Maha Suci Tuhanku Yang Maha Agung),' dan dalam sujudnya: 'Subhana Rabbiya al-A'la (Maha Suci Tuhanku Yang Maha Tinggi).' Dan setiap kali beliau melewati ayat rahmat, beliau berhenti dan memohon, dan setiap kali melewati ayat azab, beliau berlindung kepada Allah.

417.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ come to a rubbish heap belonging to a people and urinate while standing.'
417.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Aku melihat Rasulullah ﷺ mendatangi tempat sampah milik suatu kaum dan buang air kecil sambil berdiri.'

418.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The two ends of the Prophet's ﷺ Fountain are as far apart as the distance between Ayla and Mudar. Its vessels are more numerous than — or like — the count of the stars of the sky. Its water is sweeter than honey, whiter than milk, colder than snow, and more fragrant than musk. Whoever drinks from it will never be thirsty again forever.'
418.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Dua ujung telaga Nabi ﷺ sejauh jarak antara Ailah dan Mudhar. Bejananya lebih banyak dari — atau seperti — jumlah bintang-bintang di langit. Airnya lebih manis dari madu, lebih putih dari susu, lebih dingin dari salju, dan lebih harum dari misk. Barangsiapa minum darinya tidak akan pernah haus lagi selamanya.'

419.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Do not say: "What Allah wills and so-and-so wills." Rather say: "What Allah wills, then what so-and-so wills."'
419.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Janganlah kalian mengucapkan: "Apa yang dikehendaki Allah dan si fulan." Akan tetapi katakanlah: "Apa yang dikehendaki Allah, kemudian apa yang dikehendaki si fulan."'

420.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Thawr that he said: 'Uthman sent Said ibn al-As on the day of al-Jara'a. The people went out to him and turned him back. I was sitting with Abu Masud and Hudhayfa. Abu Masud said: "I did not think he would return without bloodshed." Hudhayfa said: "But I knew it — it would turn back on its heels without even a cupping's worth of blood being shed. And I knew nothing of that except while Muhammad ﷺ was alive."'
420.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Tsaur bahwa dia berkata: 'Utsman mengirim Said ibn al-Ash pada hari al-Jara'ah. Orang-orang keluar menemuinya dan mengembalikannya. Aku sedang duduk bersama Abu Masud dan Hudzaifah. Abu Masud berkata: "Aku tidak mengira dia akan kembali tanpa pertumpahan darah." Hudzaifah berkata: "Tetapi aku tahu — ia akan berbalik tanpa setetes darah pun yang tertumpah. Dan aku mengetahui ini sewaktu Muhammad ﷺ masih hidup."'

421.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) through Amr ibn Hanzala that he said: 'By Allah, Mudar will not leave a believing servant of Allah except that they will corrupt him, kill him, or Allah and the angels and the believers will strike them until they cannot protect even the tail of a valley stream.' A man said to him: 'Do you say this, O Abdullah, when you yourself are a man of Mudar?' He said: 'I only say what the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said.'
421.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melalui Amr ibn Handzalah bahwa dia berkata: 'Demi Allah, Mudhar tidak akan meninggalkan seorang hamba Allah yang beriman kecuali mereka akan merusaknya, membunuhnya, atau Allah dan para malaikat dan orang-orang beriman akan menghancurkan mereka hingga mereka tidak mampu melindungi bahkan ekor aliran lembah.' Seorang laki-laki berkata kepadanya: 'Apakah kamu mengatakan ini, wahai Abdullah, sementara kamu sendiri adalah orang dari Mudhar?' Dia berkata: 'Aku hanya mengucapkan apa yang dikatakan Rasulullah ﷺ.'

422.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) through Abd al-Rahman ibn Yazid who said: 'We said to Hudhayfa: "Tell us of a man close in conduct and bearing to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ so that we may learn from him." He said: "I do not know anyone closer in bearing, conduct, and demeanor to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ until the wall of his house hides him from view than Ibn Umm Abd." And the noble Companions of Muhammad ﷺ know that Ibn Umm Abd is among the closest of them...'
422.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melalui Abd al-Rahman ibn Yazid yang berkata: 'Kami berkata kepada Hudzaifah: "Beritahukan kepada kami tentang seorang laki-laki yang dekat dalam perilaku dan penampilan dengan Rasulullah ﷺ agar kami bisa belajar darinya." Dia berkata: "Aku tidak mengetahui siapa pun yang lebih dekat dalam penampilan, perilaku, dan sikap dengan Rasulullah ﷺ hingga tembok rumahnya menyembunyikannya dari pandangan daripada Ibn Umm Abd." Dan para Sahabat Muhammad ﷺ yang terpilih tahu bahwa Ibn Umm Abd adalah yang paling dekat di antara mereka...'

423.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mukhmil ibn Dimaths that he said: 'I went on a military expedition with Said ibn al-As. He asked the people: "Who among you witnessed the Fear Prayer with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ?" Hudhayfa said: "I did." He described how the Prophet ﷺ led one group in one rak'ah while another group faced the enemy, then those who had prayed went to face the enemy in place of their companions, and the other group came and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ led them in one rak'ah.'
423.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mukhmil ibn Dimats bahwa dia berkata: 'Aku berperang bersama Said ibn al-Ash. Dia bertanya kepada orang-orang: "Siapa di antara kalian yang menyaksikan Shalat Khauf bersama Rasulullah ﷺ?" Hudzaifah berkata: "Aku." Dia menggambarkan bagaimana Nabi ﷺ memimpin satu kelompok dalam satu rakaat sementara kelompok lain menghadapi musuh, kemudian mereka yang telah shalat pergi menggantikan posisi teman-teman mereka menghadapi musuh, dan kelompok lain datang dan Rasulullah ﷺ memimpin mereka dalam satu rakaat.'

424.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that Uqba ibn Amr said to him: 'Will you not tell us what you heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say?' He said: 'I heard him say: "When the Dajjal emerges, he will have with him water and fire. What people see as fire is cold water, and what people see as water is a burning fire. Whoever among you encounters that, let him fall into what he sees as fire, for it is sweet cold water."'
424.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Uqbah ibn Amr berkata kepadanya: 'Tidakkah engkau menceritakan kepada kami apa yang engkau dengar dari Rasulullah ﷺ?' Dia berkata: 'Aku mendengar beliau bersabda: "Ketika Dajjal muncul, bersamanya akan ada air dan api. Apa yang orang-orang lihat sebagai api adalah air yang dingin, dan apa yang orang-orang lihat sebagai air adalah api yang membakar. Siapa pun di antara kalian yang menjumpai itu, hendaklah dia jatuh ke dalam yang tampak seperti api, karena sesungguhnya itu adalah air segar yang dingin."'

425.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'And I heard him say: "A man from those before you was approached by an angel to take his soul. He was asked: "Have you done any good?" He said: "I do not know." He was told: "Think." He said: "I know of nothing except that I used to trade with people and be lenient with them, giving time to those in difficulty and being easy with those who were well-off." So Allah Almighty admitted him into Paradise."' Uqba ibn Amr said: 'I too heard him say that, and he was a cloth dealer.'
425.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Dan aku mendengar beliau bersabda: "Seorang laki-laki dari orang-orang sebelum kalian didatangi malaikat untuk mencabut jiwanya. Dia ditanya: "Apakah kamu telah melakukan kebaikan?" Dia berkata: "Aku tidak tahu." Dikatakan kepadanya: "Pikirkanlah." Dia berkata: "Aku tidak mengetahui sesuatu kecuali bahwa aku biasa berdagang dengan orang-orang dan bersikap lunak kepada mereka, memberi waktu kepada yang kesulitan dan memudahkan yang berkecukupan." Maka Allah Azza wa Jalla memasukkannya ke dalam surga."' Uqbah ibn Amr berkata: 'Aku juga mendengar beliau mengatakan itu, dan dia adalah seorang pedagang kain.'

426.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'And I heard him say: "A man who was near death, when he despaired of life, instructed his family: When I die, gather much firewood for me, then kindle it. When it has consumed my flesh and reached my bones and they have charred, take them and scatter them in the sea. They did so, and Allah Almighty gathered him and said to him: Why did you do that? He said: Out of fear of You. So Allah forgave him."' Uqba ibn Amr said: 'I heard him say that, and he was a grave-robber.'
426.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Dan aku mendengar beliau bersabda: "Seorang laki-laki yang mendekati kematian, ketika dia putus asa dari kehidupan, mewasiatkan kepada keluarganya: Ketika aku mati, kumpulkan banyak kayu bakar untukku, lalu bakarlah. Ketika api telah menghabiskan dagingku dan mencapai tulangku dan mereka telah hangus, ambillah dan taburkan ke laut. Mereka melakukannya, dan Allah Azza wa Jalla mengumpulkan dia dan berkata kepadanya: Mengapa kamu melakukan itu? Dia berkata: Karena takut kepada-Mu. Maka Allah mengampuninya."' Uqbah ibn Amr berkata: 'Aku mendengar beliau mengatakan itu, dan dia adalah seorang penggali kubur.'

427.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The only thing that prevented me from attending Badr was that I went out with my father Husayl and the disbelievers of Quraysh captured us. They said: "You intend to go to Muhammad." We said: "We intend only to go to Madinah." They took from us a covenant by Allah and His pledge that we would return to Madinah and not fight alongside him. We came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and informed him...' and he mentioned that the Prophet ﷺ honoured their pledge.
427.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Tidak ada yang menghalangiku untuk hadir di Badr kecuali bahwa aku pergi bersama ayahku Husail dan orang-orang kafir Quraisy menangkap kami. Mereka berkata: "Kalian bermaksud pergi kepada Muhammad." Kami berkata: "Kami hanya bermaksud pergi ke Madinah." Mereka mengambil dari kami perjanjian kepada Allah dan ikrar-Nya bahwa kami akan kembali ke Madinah dan tidak berperang bersamanya. Kami mendatangi Rasulullah ﷺ dan memberitahu beliau...' dan dia menyebutkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ menghormati janji mereka.

428.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) that he came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: 'While I was praying I heard a speaker saying: "O Allah, all praise is Yours, all dominion is Yours, all good is in Your hand, and to You all affairs return, their outward and inward. You are worthy of all praise, for You have power over all things. O Allah, forgive me all of my past sins, preserve me in whatever remains of my life, and grant me righteous deeds with which You are pleased."'
428.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia datang kepada Nabi ﷺ dan berkata: 'Sementara aku shalat, aku mendengar seseorang berkata: "Ya Allah, segala puji bagi-Mu, segala kerajaan milik-Mu, segala kebaikan di tangan-Mu, dan kepada-Mu semua urusan dikembalikan, yang lahir dan yang batin. Engkau layak untuk dipuji, karena Engkau Maha Kuasa atas segala sesuatu. Ya Allah, ampunilah aku semua dosa-dosaku yang lalu, jagalah aku dalam sisa hidupku, dan anugerahkan kepadaku amal-amal yang saleh yang Engkau ridhai."'

429.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ took hold of the muscle of his calf — or of Hudhayfa's calf — and said: 'The lower garment should be here. If you refuse, then here. If you still refuse, then the lower garment has no right to cover the anklebones,' or: 'the anklebones have no right to be covered by the lower garment.'
429.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ memegang otot betis beliau — atau betis Hudzaifah — dan bersabda: 'Kain sarung seharusnya di sini. Jika kamu menolak, maka di sini. Jika kamu masih menolak, maka kain sarung tidak berhak menutupi mata kaki,' atau: 'mata kaki tidak berhak ditutupi oleh kain sarung.'

430.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) through Ibn Abi Layla that Hudhayfa was in al-Mada'in when a village headman brought him a silver cup. He took it and threw it at him, saying: 'I only did this because I had already forbidden him and he did not stop. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade me from drinking from vessels of gold and silver, and from silk and brocade, and said: "They are for them in this world and for you in the Hereafter."'
430.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melalui Ibn Abi Laila bahwa Hudzaifah berada di al-Mada'in ketika seorang kepala desa membawanya cangkir dari perak. Dia mengambilnya dan melemparkannya, berkata: 'Aku hanya melakukan ini karena aku sudah melarangnya dan dia tidak berhenti. Rasulullah ﷺ melarangku dari minum dari bejana emas dan perak, dan dari sutra dan brocade, dan bersabda: "Itu untuk mereka di dunia dan untuk kalian di akhirat."'

431.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'In my nation there will be liars and impostors — twenty-seven of them, four of whom are women. And I am the Seal of the Prophets; there is no prophet after me.'
431.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Di antara umatku akan ada para pendusta dan penipu — dua puluh tujuh orang, empat di antaranya adalah perempuan. Dan aku adalah Khatam al-Nabiyyin (Penutup para Nabi); tidak ada nabi setelahku.'

432.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he heard of a man spreading gossip. He said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "A gossip-monger will not enter Paradise."'
432.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia mendengar tentang seorang laki-laki yang menyebarkan gossip. Dia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Orang yang suka mengadu domba tidak akan masuk surga."'

433.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he saw a man who did not complete his bowing or prostration. When the man finished his prayer, Hudhayfa called him and asked: 'How long have you been praying like this?' He said: 'Such and such time.' Hudhayfa said: 'You have not prayed' — or he said: 'You have not prayed to Allah' — and he thought Hudhayfa also said: 'If you die, you will die on other than the Sunnah of Muhammad ﷺ.'
433.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia melihat seorang laki-laki yang tidak menyempurnakan ruku dan sujudnya. Ketika laki-laki itu selesai shalatnya, Hudzaifah memanggilnya dan bertanya: 'Sudah berapa lama kamu shalat seperti ini?' Dia berkata: 'Sekian dan sekian waktu.' Hudzaifah berkata: 'Kamu tidak shalat' — atau dia berkata: 'Kamu tidak shalat kepada Allah' — dan dia mengira Hudzaifah juga berkata: 'Jika kamu mati, kamu akan mati bukan di atas Sunnah Muhammad ﷺ.'

434.iwh_en: It was narrated from Zirr ibn Hubaysh that he said: 'I had the pre-dawn meal then set off to the mosque. I passed by the home of Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman and entered upon him. He ordered a milch camel to be milked and a pot to be heated, then said: "Come and eat." I said: "I intend to fast." He said: "And I intend to fast," so we ate and drank. Then we went to the mosque and the prayer was established. Hudhayfa then said: "The Messenger of Allah ﷺ did the same with me." I asked: "After the Fajr prayer?" He said: "Yes — it was the Fajr prayer."'
434.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Zirr ibn Hubaysh bahwa dia berkata: 'Aku makan sahur kemudian berangkat ke masjid. Aku melewati rumah Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman dan memasukinya. Dia memerintahkan unta perahan untuk diperah dan periuk untuk dipanaskan, lalu berkata: "Kemarilah dan makanlah." Aku berkata: "Aku bermaksud puasa." Dia berkata: "Dan aku pun bermaksud puasa," maka kami makan dan minum. Kemudian kami pergi ke masjid dan shalat didirikan. Hudzaifah kemudian berkata: "Rasulullah ﷺ melakukan hal yang sama kepadaku." Aku bertanya: "Setelah shalat Fajar?" Dia berkata: "Ya — itulah shalat Fajar."'

435.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said to the Prophet ﷺ: 'O Messenger of Allah, my tongue is sharp and it is mostly directed at my family.' The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Where are you regarding seeking forgiveness? I seek forgiveness one hundred times in the day and night' — or: 'in the day.'
435.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata kepada Nabi ﷺ: 'Wahai Rasulullah, lidahku tajam dan kebanyakannya tertuju kepada keluargaku.' Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Di mana posisimu dalam hal memohon ampun? Aku memohon ampun seratus kali dalam sehari semalam' — atau: 'dalam sehari.'

436.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) through a cousin of his that Hudhayfa said: 'I stood beside the Messenger of Allah ﷺ one night and he recited the seven long surahs in seven rak'ahs. When he raised his head from bowing he would say: "Allah hears whoever praises Him," then: "All praise is due to Allah, Lord of dominion, might, majesty, and greatness." His bowing was similar in length to his standing, and his prostration was similar in length to his bowing. He finished his prayer and my legs had nearly broken.'
436.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melalui seorang sepupunya bahwa Hudzaifah berkata: 'Aku berdiri di samping Rasulullah ﷺ suatu malam dan beliau membaca tujuh surah panjang dalam tujuh rakaat. Ketika beliau mengangkat kepalanya dari ruku beliau mengucapkan: "Sami'allahu liman hamidah (Allah mendengar siapa yang memuji-Nya)," kemudian: "Segala puji bagi Allah, Pemilik kerajaan, keperkasaan, kebesaran, dan keagungan." Rukuknya serupa panjangnya dengan berdirinya, dan sujudnya serupa panjangnya dengan rukuknya. Beliau menyelesaikan shalatnya dan kedua kakiku hampir patah.'

437.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) through Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Layla who said: 'I went out with Hudhayfa to one of the agricultural areas and he asked for water to drink. A village headman brought him a silver vessel. He threw it in the man's face. We said: "Be quiet, be quiet — if we ask him about it he will not tell us." So we were quiet. Later he said: "Do you know why I threw it at him?" We said: "No." He said: "I had forbidden him from it," and he mentioned the Prophet ﷺ and the prohibition on gold and silver vessels.'
437.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melalui Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Laila yang berkata: 'Aku pergi bersama Hudzaifah ke salah satu daerah pertanian dan dia meminta air untuk diminum. Seorang kepala desa membawanya bejana perak. Dia melemparkannya ke muka orang itu. Kami berkata: "Diamlah, diamlah — jika kita bertanya kepadanya tentang itu, dia tidak akan memberitahu kita." Maka kami diam. Kemudian dia berkata: "Apakah kalian tahu mengapa aku melemparnya kepadanya?" Kami berkata: "Tidak." Dia berkata: "Aku telah melarangnya dari itu," dan dia menyebutkan Nabi ﷺ dan larangan bejana emas dan perak.'

438.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The Dajjal is blind in the left eye, with thick, bushy hair. With him are a paradise and a fire: his fire is actually paradise, and his paradise is actually fire.'
438.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Dajjal buta pada mata kirinya, dengan rambut yang lebat dan keriting. Bersamanya ada surga dan neraka: nerakanya sebenarnya adalah surga, dan surganya sebenarnya adalah neraka.'

439.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to clean his mouth when he rose at night.' Ibn Numayr said he asked al-A'mash: 'With the toothstick?' He said: 'Yes.'
439.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ biasa membersihkan mulutnya ketika bangun di malam hari.' Ibn Numayr berkata bahwa dia bertanya kepada al-A'mash: 'Dengan siwak?' Dia berkata: 'Ya.'

440.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I prayed with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ one night. He began with al-Baqara and I thought he would bow at one hundred verses. Then he continued and I thought he would pray a full rak'ah with it. He continued, then began al-Nisa and recited it, then began Al Imran and recited it. He recited at an unhurried pace. When he passed a verse of glorification he would glorify, when he passed a request he would ask, and when he passed a seeking of refuge he would seek refuge. Then he bowed...'
440.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Aku shalat bersama Rasulullah ﷺ suatu malam. Beliau memulai dengan al-Baqarah dan aku mengira beliau akan ruku pada seratus ayat. Kemudian beliau melanjutkan dan aku mengira beliau akan shalat satu rakaat penuh dengannya. Beliau melanjutkan, kemudian memulai al-Nisa dan membacanya, kemudian memulai Ali Imran dan membacanya. Beliau membaca dengan tempo yang tenang. Ketika beliau melewati ayat tasbih, beliau bertasbih; ketika melewati permintaan, beliau memohon; dan ketika melewati permohonan perlindungan, beliau berlindung kepada Allah. Kemudian beliau ruku...'

441.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) through Hammam ibn al-Harith that he said: 'We were with Hudhayfa when it was said to him: "So-and-so carries reports to Uthman." He said: "I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: 'A tale-bearer will not enter Paradise."'
441.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melalui Hammam ibn al-Harits bahwa dia berkata: 'Kami bersama Hudzaifah ketika dikatakan kepadanya: "Si fulan membawa laporan kepada Utsman." Dia berkata: "Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Penyebar fitnah tidak akan masuk surga."'

442.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ, when he retired to his bed, would say: 'O Allah, by Your Name I die and by Your Name I live.' And when he awoke he would say: 'All praise is due to Allah who has given us life after causing us to die, and to Him is the resurrection.'
442.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ apabila berbaring di tempat tidurnya, mengucapkan: 'Ya Allah, dengan nama-Mu aku mati dan dengan nama-Mu aku hidup.' Dan ketika terbangun beliau mengucapkan: 'Segala puji bagi Allah yang telah menghidupkan kami setelah mematikan kami, dan kepada-Nya lah kebangkitan.'

443.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said — or: 'Your Prophet ﷺ said' — 'Every act of kindness is charity.'
443.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda — atau: 'Nabi kalian ﷺ bersabda' — 'Setiap perbuatan baik adalah sedekah.'

444.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I was a sharp-tongued man toward my family. I said: "O Messenger of Allah, I fear my tongue may take me into the Fire." He said: "And where are you regarding seeking forgiveness? I seek forgiveness from Allah one hundred times in the day." Abu Ishaq said: 'I mentioned this to Abu Burda who said: "And I repent to Him."'
444.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Aku adalah orang yang lidahnya tajam terhadap keluargaku. Aku berkata: "Wahai Rasulullah, aku khawatir lidahku akan membawaku ke neraka." Beliau bersabda: "Dan di mana posisimu dalam hal memohon ampun? Aku memohon ampun kepada Allah seratus kali dalam sehari."' Abu Ishaq berkata: 'Aku menyebutkan ini kepada Abu Burda yang berkata: "Dan aku bertaubat kepada-Nya."'

445.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that the polytheists captured him and his father and made them pledge not to fight against them on the day of Badr. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Honour your pledge to them, and we shall seek Allah's help against them.'
445.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa orang-orang musyrik menangkap dia dan ayahnya dan membuat mereka berjanji untuk tidak berperang melawan mereka pada hari Badr. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Penuhilah janji kalian kepada mereka, dan kita akan memohon pertolongan Allah terhadap mereka.'

446.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'We were with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ when food was brought. Then a Bedouin came rushing as if being chased and reached out to take some. The Prophet ﷺ took hold of his hand. Then a young girl came rushing as if being chased and reached out. The Prophet ﷺ took hold of her hand. He said: "The Shaytan comes with the Bedouin and the girl when you tire him out, to make the food permissible for him."'
446.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Kami bersama Rasulullah ﷺ ketika makanan dibawa. Kemudian seorang Badui datang dengan tergesa-gesa seolah sedang dikejar dan mengulurkan tangan untuk mengambil. Nabi ﷺ memegang tangannya. Kemudian seorang gadis datang dengan tergesa-gesa seolah sedang dikejar dan mengulurkan tangan. Nabi ﷺ memegang tangannya. Beliau bersabda: "Setan datang bersama orang Badui dan gadis itu ketika kalian membuatnya lelah, untuk menghalalkan makanan baginya."'

447.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) through Ibn Abi Layla that Hudhayfa asked for water to drink and someone brought him a silver vessel. He threw it at him, saying: 'I had already forbidden him and he refused to stop. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade us from drinking from vessels of gold and silver, and from wearing silk and brocade, and said: "These are for them in this world and for you in the Hereafter."'
447.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melalui Ibn Abi Laila bahwa Hudzaifah meminta air untuk diminum dan seseorang membawanya bejana perak. Dia melemparkannya kepadanya, berkata: 'Aku telah melarangnya dan dia menolak berhenti. Rasulullah ﷺ melarang kami dari minum dari bejana emas dan perak, dan dari memakai sutra dan brocade, dan bersabda: "Ini untuk mereka di dunia dan untuk kalian di akhirat."'

448.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he prayed with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ at night. When he entered the prayer he said: 'Allahu Akbar, Owner of dominion, might, majesty, and greatness.' Then he recited al-Baqara, then bowed. His bowing was similar in length to his standing, and he used to say: 'Glory be to my Lord, the Mighty.' Then he raised his head and his standing was similar in length to his bowing, and he used to say: 'To my Lord belongs all praise.' Then he prostrated and recited al-Nisa and Al Imran.
448.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia shalat bersama Rasulullah ﷺ di malam hari. Ketika masuk shalat beliau mengucapkan: 'Allahu Akbar, Pemilik kerajaan, keperkasaan, kebesaran, dan keagungan.' Kemudian beliau membaca al-Baqarah, kemudian ruku. Rukuknya serupa panjangnya dengan berdirinya, dan beliau mengucapkan: 'Subhana Rabbiya al-Adzim (Maha Suci Tuhanku Yang Maha Agung).' Kemudian beliau mengangkat kepalanya dan berdirinya serupa panjangnya dengan rukuknya, dan beliau mengucapkan: 'Lirabbiya al-hamd (Bagi Tuhanku segala puji).' Kemudian beliau sujud dan membaca al-Nisa dan Ali Imran.

449.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) through Abu Mijlaz that a man sat in the middle of a gathering circle. Hudhayfa said: 'By the tongue of Muhammad ﷺ, cursed is the one who sits in the middle of the circle,' or he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ cursed whoever sits in the middle of the circle.' Shu'ba noted that Abu Mijlaz did not meet Hudhayfa.
449.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melalui Abu Mijlaz bahwa seorang laki-laki duduk di tengah-tengah lingkaran majelis. Hudzaifah berkata: 'Dengan lisan Muhammad ﷺ, terlaknat orang yang duduk di tengah-tengah lingkaran,' atau dia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ melaknat orang yang duduk di tengah-tengah lingkaran.' Syu'bah mencatat bahwa Abu Mijlaz tidak pernah bertemu Hudzaifah.

450.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The people of Najran came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and said: "Send us a trustworthy man." He said: "I will certainly send you a man who is truly, truly trustworthy." The people looked up hoping to be chosen. He sent Abu Ubayda ibn al-Jarrah (may Allah be pleased with him).'
450.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Penduduk Najran datang kepada Rasulullah ﷺ dan berkata: "Kirimkan kepada kami seorang yang tepercaya." Beliau bersabda: "Sungguh aku akan mengirimkan kepada kalian seorang laki-laki yang benar-benar, benar-benar tepercaya." Orang-orang mendongakkan kepala berharap terpilih. Beliau mengirim Abu Ubaidah ibn al-Jarrah (radhiyallahu 'anhu).'
